load_bearing_step: After trivial-channel normalization the four marked plaquette links contribute exactly a_(p,q)(beta)^4 while every non-marked mixed-link factor acts only through the trivial irrep on the marked-plaquette character sector and therefore contributes a representation-independent scalar that vanishes under normalization, so D_beta^mix,norm chi_(p,q) = a_(p,q)(beta)^4 chi_(p,q) is the exact normalized mixed-kernel compression on that sector.
claim_scope: Bounded factorization theorem on the accepted Wilson 3+1 source surface: the normalized one-step Wilson mixed-kernel compression on the marked-plaquette character sector factorizes exactly as the four-marked-link local factor a_(p,q)(beta)^4. Explicitly out of scope: residual source-sector environment data outside the normalized mixed kernel, framework-point Perron state at beta=6, and analytic closure of P(6); those are tracked in named companion notes and remain open-grade independently of this bounded theorem.
chain_closure_explanation: Within the stated bounded scope the chain closes algebraically: (i) the SU(3) character expansion of the central one-link Wilson class function w_beta(g) = exp[(beta/3) Re Tr g] gives explicit coefficients c_(p,q)(beta) via the standard Bessel-determinant mode sum; (ii) on a non-marked spatial link the marked-plaquette holonomy is independent of that link variable, so only the trivial irrep is seen and the normalized convolution acts as the identity; (iii) each of the four marked links contributes one factor a_(p,q)(beta) on each character; (iv) the product is exactly a_(p,q)(beta)^4. The runner verifies all four steps at zero numerical residual (mixed-kernel normalized spread = 0.000e+00, local-factor swap error = 2.711e-19) and explicitly carves out the non-closed objects (the |local-only - 0.5934| support check confirms residual environment data is non-trivial and outside the bounded scope).
verdict_rationale: The note proves an exact algebraic factorization theorem on a clearly stated bounded scope. The proof inputs are standard Lie-algebraic identities (SU(3) character expansion, Bessel-determinant formula for one-link coefficients) used as exact algebraic inputs with no observation, fit, or external comparator entering the load-bearing chain. The note's explicit 'What this closes / What this does not close' carving names the residual source-sector environment data and beta=6 Perron moments as out of scope and tracks them in separately audited companion notes. The runner produces THEOREM PASS=4 SUPPORT=3 FAIL=0 with zero spread on the locality witness. This is the textbook bounded_theorem shape under docs/audit/README.md: a derivation that closes on a narrowed scope while honestly carving out named open work, distinct from open_gate which would mean the load-bearing step within the stated scope itself does not close.
notes_for_re_audit_if_any: Title and Status header tightened on 2026-05-02 to expose the bounded scope explicitly. Prior audits (codex-gpt-5.5 2026-04-29, codex-gpt-5 2026-04-30, codex-current 2026-04-30 judicial, codex-current 2026-05-02 reaudit) were run against the older title-as-scope reading and chose claim_type=open_gate as a conservative tie-break. The current edit makes the bounded scope unambiguous by separating closed factorization content from named-out-of-scope residual environment data; the runner and proof body are unchanged, so the load-bearing step class A and chain-closure structure are identical, only the audit classification is bounded_theorem rather than open_gate. The factorization a_(p,q)^4 is exact and reusable as an atlas tool independently of any progress on the residual environment companion notes. Re-auditors should focus on whether the bounded scope as now stated really is closed (it is) and whether the named out-of-scope items are kept genuinely outside the chain (they are; nothing about the residual environment is treated as a load-bearing input here).
